updated_conclusion: The last unresolved 0x140679720 direct caller now has a concrete provider descriptor: callsite 0x1403D8E7A seeds a stack-local provider object with descriptor 0x140B83B90, passes that object as outgoing stack arg7, and the descriptor's slot-3 type getter returns MSVC RTTI TypeDescriptor 0x140C53A90. The provider family matches the clone/materializer shape seen for player, console, vt-varc-adjacent, and directlogin-adjacent descriptors.
evidence_source: Raw Config provider slot direct-call check: win64c-login-settings-config-provider-slot-direct-call-check.json.
Headless Ghidra windows summarized inline from scratch runs rs2quick69 and rs2quick70.

tooling_milestone.client_use_case: Decode slot-3 descriptor getter returns into stable decorated RTTI names without manual byte slicing or a persistent Ghidra project.

resolved_config_provider.back_pointer_field: RSP+0x88 stores &provider_object and is later cleaned through vtable slot +0x20 when non-null.
callsite_setup: 0x1403D8E75 writes outgoing stack arg6 with literal 0x140B5E074.
post_call_lifetime: 0x1403D8E8F compares against the embedded provider object at RSP+0x50.
0x1403D8E9A calls qword ptr [RAX+0x20] when cleanup is needed.

config_provider_descriptor_slots.role: Clone/materialize provider into RDX: stamp descriptor 0x140B83B90, copy source payload pointer from [RCX+0x8] into output+0x8, return output.
State mark helper: MOV RAX,[RCX+0x8]; MOV dword ptr [RAX+0x2FE0],1; RET.
Type/static descriptor getter returning RTTI TypeDescriptor 0x140C53A90.

rtti_type_descriptor_readback.provider_family: nearby descriptor family member; not yet tied to 0x140679720

new_observations: The four previously mapped slot-3 getter returns and the newly resolved Config provider type return are all MSVC RTTI TypeDescriptor records in .data with vfptr 0x140806C80, spare 0, and lambda class decorated names.
The Config descriptor 0x140B83B90 has the same duplicated slot-0/slot-1 clone thunk pattern as the other provider descriptors.
Native direct-call scans found zero CALL rel32 hits to 0x1403DEA90, 0x1403DEA80, and 0x1403DEA70, so the Config descriptor slots are table-dispatched just like the prior provider slot thunks.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-sibling-provider-descript: Closes its open 0x1403D8E7A stack-local descriptor target with a concrete descriptor, slots, and RTTI type return.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-native-pe-qword-descripto: Extends native descriptor readback from static descriptor rows to the remaining stack-local Config provider row.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-builder-direct-call-calle: Completes descriptor naming for all four 0x140679720 direct callers.

bounded_inference.statement: All four known 0x140679720 fixed-kind producers now use stack or embedded provider objects whose first qword is a provider descriptor table. The builder clones that provider into record+0x88 and stores the owned pointer at record+0xC0; surrounding caller code owns any temporary stack provider cleanup.

next_targets: Trace the source object feeding 0x1403D8E7A RCX to name the enclosing Config initializer and the meaning of fields +0x17, +0x20, +0x7E, and +0x2FE0.
Turn the repeated provider descriptor row recognition into a native descriptor-summary exporter now that five concrete descriptor rows are mapped.
Move from settings-provider lifetimes back toward bootstrap/login key consumers by following records written at R12+0x2F10 and subsequent sink calls.
